<task-notification>
<task-id>a5cbe55e323a4a24c</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_014YJXTFhepy6ajZP8p1cjzZ</tool-use-id>
<output-file>C:\Users\bogwe\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\F--dev-clair\811e8325-3960-4a7d-893e-728ba3e94faf\tasks\a5cbe55e323a4a24c.output</output-file>

<summary>Agent "Review runner Task 1" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>Now I have everything I need. Here is the review.

---

## 1. Spec Compliance: ã

**Files required vs. delivered:**

| Requirement | Status |
|---|---|
| `loadRun.ts`, `sliceSpec.ts`, `types.ts` | ã All created |
| `__tests__/sliceSpec.test.ts` | ã Created |
| `loadRun(path): RunConfig` | ã `loadRun.ts:4ã5` |
| `buildSliceSpecs(run, backlogPath): SliceSpec[]` | ã `sliceSpec.ts:115` |
| `SliceSpec = { id, title, prompt }` | ã `types.ts:43ã46` |
| Prompt contains title + rationale + ACs | ã `buildPrompt` includes both |
| Explicit instructions: worktree / write tests / commit / never block | ã `sliceSpec.ts:81ã85` |
| Touch-set as hints, not commands | ã "Hint files (touch-set)" label, `sliceSpec.ts:105` |
| Test: 3 specs with S1/S2/S3 IDs | ã `sliceSpec.test.ts:46ã84` |
| **Information asymmetry ã both directions** | ã fully covered (see below) |

**Asymmetry coverage in tests:**

The brief required bidirectional tests. What's present:
- S1 contains F-08 AC ("viewer-role JWT") ã `test.ts:87ã93`
- S1 NOT F-06 ("microscope") ã `test.ts:103ã109`
- S1 NOT F-17 ("text/csv") ã `test.ts:111ã117`
- S2 contains F-06 ("microscope") ã `test.ts:119ã124`
- S3 contains F-17 ("text/csv") ã `test.ts:126ã131`
- S2 NOT F-08 ("requireRole") ã `test.ts:133ã139`
- S3 NOT F-06 ("microscope") ã `test.ts:141ã146`

Both directions for S1 (the brief's stated requirement) are covered. The additional S2/S3 exclusion tests are a bonus but the matrix has two gaps (S2 not S3, S3 not S1) ã not required by the brief, so not a deficiency.

**No YAGNI violations in behavior** ã no extra flags, no runtime abstractions beyond the brief.

---

## 2. Code Quality: Approved ã with findings

### Minor

**`sliceSpec.ts:8` ã dead `sectionPattern` variable.**
```ts
const sectionPattern = /^### `([A-Z]+-\d+)` ã (.+)$/m;
```
Defined at line 1468 of the diff, never referenced. The actual match at line 1473 uses a separate inline literal. This is dead code and a reader distraction. Harmless but should be removed. TypeScript won't catch it without `"noUnusedLocals": true` in tsconfig.

**`types.ts:15ã40` ã speculative typing of full `RunConfig`.**
Fields like `arm`, `topology`, `gate`, `integration`, `trials`, `metrics` are not accessed by task 1. Modeling the full YAML schema upfront has value for the project but is technically beyond the task scope. Acceptable here given the sub-project context; call it out for awareness.

**`sliceSpec.test.ts:90, 98, 134, 143` ã bare `!` non-null assertions.**
```ts
const s1 = specs.find(s => s.id === "S1")!;
expect(s1.prompt).toContain("viewer-role JWT");
```
If `find` returns `undefined` (e.g., a regression makes S1 disappear), this produces `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined` rather than a clean test failure. The prior test asserting three correct IDs reduces the risk, but a `expect(s1).toBeDefined()` guard before `.prompt` access is cleaner.

### Important (robustness)

**`sliceSpec.ts:52` ã silent AC extraction failure.**
```ts
const acMatch = part.match(
  /\*\*Acceptance criteria \(behavioral ã gate material\):\*\*\n((?:\s+-\s+.+\n?)+)/
);
```
The literal em-dash `ã` (U+2014) must match exactly. If the backlog header text changes (even slightly ã e.g., casing, wording, or encoding artifact), `acMatch` is `null` and `acceptanceCriteria` is silently `[]`. No error is thrown, and a prompt built with empty ACs still passes the asymmetry tests (which check for presence of specific strings from the *actual* backlog content). The report acknowledges format brittleness but doesn't flag the silent failure mode. At minimum a warning log or a post-parse assert (`if (acceptanceCriteria.length === 0) throw`) would surface the problem without requiring a manual diff.

---

## 3. Cannot verify from diff

- **`benchmark/runs/standard-L1.run.yaml` and `benchmark/backlog/backlog.md`** ã the tests resolve these at runtime against the actual repo files. I cannot confirm from the diff alone that those files have the exact strings the tests assert (`viewer-role JWT`, `requireRole`, `microscope`, `text/csv`). The 19-pass report from `pnpm test` is the evidence; trust it unless the run config or backlog was modified after the test was written.
- **`loadRun` type safety at runtime** ã `parse(raw) as RunConfig` (loadRun.ts:5) is an unchecked cast; the types assume the YAML matches the schema. No runtime validation exists. This will produce confusing errors if the YAML is missing a required field, but it is outside the stated scope of task 1.

---

**Bottom line:** Spec is fully met. The dead `sectionPattern` variable and silent AC-parse failure on header mismatch are the two things worth fixing before downstream tasks rely on this; everything else is minor style. The asymmetry tests are substantive and would genuinely fail without the isolation logic.</result>

author: JB
text:
<task-notification>
<task-id>aa0a34e65ee372ccf</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_019Vs1bwuxp1MEgzBxJkxvh5</tool-use-id>
<output-file>C:\Users\bogwe\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\F--dev-clair\811e8325-3960-4a7d-893e-728ba3e94faf\tasks\aa0a34e65ee372ccf.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Review runner Task 2" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>## 1. Spec compliance

| Requirement | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Create `benchmark/runner/workspace.ts` | ã | diff: new file, 83 lines |
| Create `benchmark/runner/__tests__/workspace.test.ts` | ã | diff: new file, 118 lines |
| `provision(run)` ã one worktree per slice on new branch `run/<runId>/<sliceId>` off `arena/base` | ã | workspace.ts:199 |
| `pnpm install` in each worktree | ã | workspace.ts:201-203, gated by `install` opt (false in tests) |
| Returns `Workspace[]` with paths + branch names | ã | workspace.ts:156-160, 205 |
| `teardown(workspaces)` | ã | workspace.ts:215-233 |
| Test: N slices ã N distinct dirs, each on its own branch off `arena/base` | ã | test:76-88 (dirs), test:90-100 (SHA check) |
| Test: teardown removes them and prunes | ã | test:102-116 |
| Test: re-provision with same runId is clean | ã | test:118-133 |

**Extra (not in brief, not harmful):** Double-teardown idempotency test (test:135-143), `.gitignore` addition.

**Missing:** Nothing from the brief is absent.

---

## 2. Code quality: Approved ã with findings

### Important

**workspace.ts:152 ã `execSync` used for `pnpm install`, inconsistent with `execFileSync` everywhere else.**
`execSync("pnpm install", ...)` goes through the shell; the rest of the file uses `execFileSync`. With a hardcoded string there is no injection risk, but the inconsistency is gratuitous. Use `execFileSync("pnpm", ["install"], { cwd: dir, stdio: "inherit" })`.

**test:118-133 ã `ws1` is outside the `afterEach` guard.**
The idempotency test provisions `ws1` and assigns it to a local (not to `workspaces`). If `teardown(ws1)` throws unexpectedly, those 3 worktrees and branches are not cleaned up by `afterEach` because `workspaces` is still `[]` at the point of failure. Fix: assign `ws1` to `workspaces` immediately after provision and clear it only after the manual teardown succeeds:

```ts
workspaces = await provision(run, { rootDir: tmpDir, install: false });
await teardown(workspaces);
workspaces = []; // now safe
workspaces = await provision(run, { rootDir: tmpDir2, install: false });
```

### Minor

**test:71 ã `afterEach` swallows all teardown errors silently.**
`.catch(() => {})` means a teardown that fails for an unexpected reason (not "already removed") leaves stray git state without any diagnostic. At minimum: `.catch(e => console.error("[afterEach] teardown failed:", e))`. This does not affect correctness of passing tests but obscures leaks during development.

**test:125 ã `tmpDir2` is not cleaned up.**
Not a git leak, but the OS temp dir is left behind. Low severity.

**workspace.ts:167,169 ã `REPO_ROOT` / `DEFAULT_WORK_DIR` fragile under compilation.**
Both are resolved from `__dirname` at runtime. In the Vitest/tsx context `__dirname` = `benchmark/runner` and both resolve correctly. If the source is ever compiled to `dist/`, `path.resolve(__dirname, "../..")` resolves to `benchmark/` (not repo root). Works today; would silently break on a compile-and-run path. Add a comment noting this assumption.

---

## 3. Cross-task / cannot verify from diff

**`teardown` permanently destroys slice branches (`git branch -D run/<runId>/<sliceId>`).** This is required for idempotency (re-provision with the same `runId`), and the implementation is correct in isolation. However, it destroys all agent-committed work on those branches. **The controller must invoke scoring/merge before calling `teardown`** ã if it calls `teardown` first, the benchmark output is gone. This is a cross-task ordering constraint that Task 2 cannot enforce itself; it must be documented as a precondition for the controller (Task 3 or equivalent). Flag this in the controller's brief.

**`arena/base` must exist in the repo.** Tests fail at the first `git worktree add` if it does not. Verified correct in the implementer's passing run, but not visible in this diff.

**`standard-L1.run.yaml` slice count.** Tests assert `toHaveLength(3)`; the slice count is drawn from the run config (Task 1 territory). Correct per the report but unverifiable here.</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>24948</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>3</tool_uses><duration_ms>109364</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>

<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>**Status:** DONE

**Commit:** `7ebb333` on `feat/benchmark-runner`

**Test summary:** 37 passed (12 new + 25 existing) ã `pnpm test` green in ~2.4s, no real `claude` process or git worktree touched.

**Key decisions:**
- `Budget` interface includes `model: string` (otherwise the default `RunClaudeFn` has no model to pass to claude)
- `deps` extended to `{ runClaude?, checkCommitted? }` ã injecting the committed-check is cleaner than provisioning real worktrees in tests
- `didNotComplete` fires on any of: `exit !== 0`, `is_error` in JSON, `turns >= cap`, `tokens > cap`, or `!committed`
- JSON parsing fully defensive with `typeof` guards + try/catch; missing fields ã 0, never throws

**Concerns:**
1. `claude -p --output-format json` field names (`num_turns`, `usage.input_tokens`, etc.) are inferred from the spec ã should be validated against a live run before trusting production numbers
2. On Windows `spawn("claude")` may need `{ shell: true }` if claude ships as a `.cmd` wrapper ã the `error` handler returns `exit:1` rather than throwing, so it degrades gracefully</result>

author: JB
text:
<task-notification>
<task-id>af7689ad2ec501cdf</task-id>
<tool-use-id>toolu_0192bY9CZyhkvQY9BCm8CBi2</tool-use-id>
<output-file>C:\Users\bogwe\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\F--dev-clair\811e8325-3960-4a7d-893e-728ba3e94faf\tasks\af7689ad2ec501cdf.output</output-file>
<status>completed</status>
<summary>Agent "Review runner Task 3" finished</summary>
<note>A task-notification fires each time this agent stops with no live background children of its own. The user can send it another message and resume it, so the same task-id may notify more than once.</note>
<result>## 1. Spec Compliance

| Requirement | Evidence | Status |
|---|---|---|
| `runAgent(workspace, spec, budget, deps?)` signature | `agent.ts:419` ã exact match | ã |
| `AgentResult` has `sliceId, committed, tokens, turns, wallMs, exit` | `agent.ts:315ã322` | ã |
| `didNotComplete` field (implied by brief's test list: "did-not-complete flag") | `agent.ts:322` | ã |
| Spawns `claude -p` with pre-granted permissions (`--dangerously-skip-permissions`) | `agent.ts:364ã373` | ã |
| Budget cap enforced (`--max-turns`) | `agent.ts:371` | ã |
| `committed` true iff HEAD ã  `arena/base` | `agent.ts:398ã409` | ã |
| Tests use injected `runClaude` and `checkCommitted` ã no real `claude` or `git` | `agent.test.ts:66ã78`; every `describe` block passes `fakeRun`/`fakeCommitted` | ã |
| Defensive JSON parsing: never throws, missing ã 0 | `agent.ts:441ã452` (`try/catch` + `typeof` guards) | ã |
| `didNotComplete`: `exitã 0` | `agent.ts:457` | ã |
| `didNotComplete`: `is_error` | `agent.ts:458` | ã |
| `didNotComplete`: `turns >= cap` (equality included) | `agent.ts:459` | ã |
| `didNotComplete`: `tokens > cap` (strict greater-than) | `agent.ts:460` | ã |
| `didNotComplete`: `!committed` | `agent.ts:461` | ã |
| `runAgents` via `Promise.all`, preserves input order | `agent.ts:482ã485` | ã |
| Tests: committed run ã `committed:true`, parsed tokens/turns | `agent.test.ts:85ã98` | ã |
| Tests: budget exceeded ã `didNotComplete:true` | `agent.test.ts:110ã130` | ã |
| Tests: 3 agents concurrent | `agent.test.ts:224ã253` | ã |

**Missing from brief:** Nothing missing.
**Extra beyond brief:** `didNotComplete` on `AgentResult` (correct addition). `Budget.model` field (reasonable ã default `RunClaudeFn` needs it). Three additional tests beyond the brief's list (`empty-JSON defensive`, `argv pass-through check`, `partial-failure isolation`) ã all high-quality, no dead weight.

---

## 2. Code Quality: Approved ã with one Important flag

### Important

**Windows: `spawn("claude")` will silently fail on every live run ã `agent.ts:363`**

On Windows, `claude` installed via npm is a `.cmd` wrapper. `child_process.spawn("claude", ...)` without `{ shell: true }` fires `ENOENT`; the `error` handler at `agent.ts:386ã388` catches it and resolves `{ stdout: "", exit: 1 }`. Every live run on Windows will therefore return `exit:1, didNotComplete:true` with no indication that spawn itself failed ã not a parse failure, not a claude error. The report raises this but leaves it unfixed. When the live-run task arrives, callers on Windows will need to override `runClaude` or add `{ shell: true }` to the default. The current code is not broken for CI-as-Linux, but the silent degradation path is dangerous: a Windows developer running the benchmark gets all `didNotComplete` results that look like agent failures rather than an environment misconfiguration.

### Minor

**`defaultCheckCommitted` blocks the event loop ã `agent.ts:397ã409`**

`execFileSync` is synchronous inside an `async` function. When N agents finish nearly simultaneously, their `checkCommitted` calls run serially on the main thread. At N=3 this is probably a few tens of milliseconds; at larger concurrency it serializes git calls that could otherwise run concurrently. The report documents this as deliberate simplicity. Flag for the live-run task.

**No assertion that `checkCommitted` receives the correct `workspace` ã `agent.test.ts:85ã191`**

The `fakeCommitted` helper ignores its argument (`_ws`). No test verifies that the `workspace` object passed to `checkCommitted` is the one belonging to the current slice, not a cross-contaminated one. Low risk given the simple pass-through at `agent.ts:454`, but the injection contract is only half-tested.

**Beyond-brief test at `agent.test.ts:256ã279`**

"Returns independent results even when some agents fail" ã not in the brief's test list but tests a real property (`Promise.all` doesn't reject-short-circuit because `runAgent` never rejects). Useful, not bloat.

---

## 3. Cannot Verify from Diff

- **Real `claude -p --output-format json` field names.** The parser targets `num_turns`, `usage.input_tokens`, `usage.output_tokens`, `is_error`. If a live run emits different names (e.g., `total_turns`, `inputTokens`), all values will silently be 0. Must verify against an actual `claude` invocation before trusting benchmark numbers.
- **Windows `.cmd` spawn behavior** ã structural concern analysed above; only observable on a Windows live run.
- **`arena/base` ref existence in real worktrees.** `defaultCheckCommitted` returns `false` on any git error, so a missing ref reads as `committed:false, didNotComplete:true` rather than an exception. Correct behavior, but needs the ref to be established before benchmarks run.</result>
<usage><subagent_tokens>28795</subagent_tokens><tool_uses>3</tool_uses><duration_ms>145158</duration_ms></usage>
</task-notification>
